ops.title: Deferred grep/rg guard review findings (pre-existing / native-parity)
ops.body:
Adversarial review (codex finder + fable refuter) of the grep/rg guard-softening change surfaced findings consciously deferred out of that PR because they are pre-existing rewrite-lane properties or native-tool parity, not regressions the change introduced:

- **Exit-code divergence in grep→ccx rewrites** (finder #8): `grep -r ABSENT . && echo` — native grep exits 1 (no match) skipping the `&&`; ccx exits 0 and runs it. Applies to ALL grep→ccx rewrites, predates this change. Faithful fix = make ccx propagate grep's no-match exit semantics — a separate design.
- **Non-recursive grep on a dir widens to recursive** (finder #9): `grep foo .` (no -r) already rewrote to repo-wide ccx before this change; native grep errors "Is a directory". Pre-existing.
- **codeexec bool flags keyword-only despite positional signature** (finder #12): uniform across regex/word/ignore_case too; a codeexec-wide arg-binding property, not new. Fix = teach codeexec `args` positional-bool binding.
- **`-l` line-based parsing breaks on newline-in-filename** (#10) and **no-match `# …` record** (#14): both match native `grep -l`/`rg -l` limitations and ccx's diagnostic convention. Accepted as native parity.

Fixed in the shipping change: broken `-l`+context rewrite (#6), conditional-cd cwd-trust (#5/GAP C), downstream-lane fail-closed on unparseable operands + path_blocked screen (#16/GAP D), sink-count cap (GAP B), intermediate-tee decline (GAP A), context-flag fidelity in appendContext (#7), budget mid-path truncation (#15).</body>
<anchor_paths>["plugin/capt-hook/hooks/grep_guards.py", "plugin/capt-hook/hooks/rg_guards.py", "internal/ripgrep/ripgrep.go"]</anchor_paths>
</invoke>
